security.title: Security

help.totp: Time-based one-time codes from an authenticator app (e.g. Google Authenticator, Authy) — a second factor in addition to your password.
help.passkey: Passkeys (WebAuthn) authenticate via your device's built-in security (fingerprint, face, or PIN) or a hardware key instead of a code you type in — generally faster and phishing-resistant.

security.scanHint: Scan this QR code with your authenticator app, then enter the 6-digit code to confirm.
security.passkeyHint: Passkeys are a secure alternative to authenticator codes (Face ID, Touch ID, security key).
security.passkeyDeleteLastError: Cannot remove the last 2FA factor while 2FA is enforced.
security.passkeyUnsupported: This browser does not support passkeys (WebAuthn).

ai.title: AI Assist

help.summary: Generated from the ticket's articles by the queue's configured model and stored only on the ticket state, never as a note — it never gets sent to the customer on its own.
help.drafts: Generated by the model from the ticket so far. Each draft is its own record — nothing is sent automatically; you must open it and explicitly send or discard it.

ai.summaryUpToDate: Already up to date — no new articles since the last summary.
ai.createDraftHint: This can take up to a minute…
ai.discardConfirm: Discard this AI draft? This cannot be undone.
ai.errorLocked: An AI run is already active for this ticket. Please try again shortly.
ai.errorForbidden: You don't have permission to use this AI feature.
ai.errorRateLimited: Limit reached — please try again later.
ai.errorDisabled: This AI feature is currently disabled.
ai.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.
ai.adminDeleteDraftHint: Admin: permanently delete this draft from the database
ai.adminDeleteConfirm: Permanently delete this AI draft? This cannot be undone.
ai.adminDeleteSummaryHint: Admin: delete the stored AI summary — the next run starts from scratch
ai.adminDeleteSummaryConfirm: Permanently delete this ticket's stored AI summary? The next run will summarize the ticket from scratch.

escalationTester.title: Rule tester
escalationTester.samplePlaceholder: Sample tool result as JSON, e.g. {"lock_code": "COPYR"}

aiQueue.queue: The queue this policy applies to. Each queue can have at most one policy; policies are not inherited by subqueues.
aiQueue.systemPrompt: Extra prompt text prepended to every model request (e.g. tone, queue-specific context) — combined with the autonomy template and any identity rules.
aiQueue.llmProvider: The language model provider for this queue. Must support tool calling once MCP tools or autonomy are used.
aiQueue.modelOverride: Overrides the selected provider's default model for this queue only.
aiQueue.visionProvider: Image attachments are never shown to the main model — only to this dedicated vision model, which describes them in plain text. That description (not the image) is what the main model sees. Leave unset to ignore image attachments entirely. Because images cannot be masked for PII, prefer an EU-hosted provider here and enable it deliberately.
aiQueue.enabledManualAssist: Shows the "AI draft" button in the ticket zoom. Drafts are their own record, never an article — an agent must explicitly accept and send each one. Not affected by the operation-mode gate.
aiQueue.kbTags: Comma-separated tags whose knowledge-base articles are given to the model as context.
aiQueue.kbCategoryIds: Comma-separated knowledge-base category IDs, included in addition to the tags.
aiQueue.mcpClients: MCP clients whose enabled tools are available to the model for this queue. Mutating tools are only allowed at "Fully autonomous".
aiQueue.enabledSummary: Shows the "Summarize" button in the ticket zoom and allows automatic summaries once the thresholds below are met. Stored exclusively in the ticket state, never as an internal note.
aiQueue.summaryArticleThreshold: Triggers an automatic re-summary once at least this many articles have arrived since the last summary.
aiQueue.summaryCharThreshold: Triggers an automatic re-summary once the new content since the last summary exceeds this many characters.
aiQueue.summaryIncrementalMinArticles: Minimum number of new articles before an incremental (rather than full) recompute is triggered.
aiQueue.summaryIncrementalMinChars: Minimum number of new characters before an incremental recompute is triggered.
aiQueue.enabledAutoReply: Enables the autonomous auto-reply worker for this queue — sends customer replies without human approval, per the autonomy matrix. Requires "Tiqora only" (tiqora_primary) mode.
aiQueue.autonomy: Controls what the AI may do without human approval: "Off" = drafts only, "Clarify only" = clarifying questions may go out directly (substantive replies stay drafts), "Fully autonomous" = both may be sent automatically, including mutating MCP tools.
aiQueue.serviceUser: The agent account under which this queue's automatic replies/clarifications are created (audit and permission identity). Required once auto replies are enabled.
aiQueue.maxClarifications: Maximum number of automatic clarifying questions per ticket before escalating.
aiQueue.maxAutoReplies: Maximum number of automatic replies per ticket.
aiQueue.maxRepliesPerHour: Maximum number of automatic replies for this queue per hour — a cost brake.
aiQueue.budgetTokensDay: Daily token budget for this queue's auto-reply path. Once exceeded, the auto-worker pauses until the next day.
aiQueue.disclosureEnabled: Appends an AI-disclosure footer to autonomously sent customer replies. Drafts an agent accepts and sends count as a human reply and get no footer.
aiQueue.disclosureText: Queue-specific disclosure text. Empty = the global default text from AI settings is used.
aiQueue.escalationRules: JSON rules that check MCP tool results before PII masking; any match hard-stops automatic sending and escalates to a human (pure OR logic, no priority between rules).
aiQueue.piiMasking: Masks recognized customer identifiers (email, phone, MAC, IP) in ticket text and MCP tool results before they reach the model, and unmasks them again before the actual tool call or send. Complements, but does not replace, provider choice (EU hosting/DPA) as the primary privacy control.
aiQueue.identityMode: How customer identity is resolved for tool calls: "Ticket customer" uses the customer ID on the ticket, "Clarify schema" lets the AI actively ask for missing information, "Off" disables identity resolution.
aiQueue.clarifySchemaJson: JSON description of the fields the AI may actively collect under the clarify schema (field name + optional MCP match tool).
aiQueue.ignoredSenders: Senders that are never auto-replied to — an exact address or a "*@domain" glob, case-insensitive. A match is a silent skip (no run, no draft). Examples: noreply@example.com, *@internal.example.
aiQueue.ignoreSendersManual: On a match, also blocks the manual "AI draft" button (409 error) — by default only the auto-reply path is affected, Manual Assist still runs for debugging.
aiQueue.replyLanguageMode: Controls whether the model is given a binding reply language: "Off" = today's behaviour (no directive), "Fixed" = always the configured language, "Auto" = detected from the subject + latest customer article, falling back to the configured default when the text is too short/ambiguous.
aiQueue.replyLanguageFixed: BCP-47 language code (e.g. "de", "en") always used as the binding reply language in "Fixed" mode.
aiQueue.replyLanguageDefault: BCP-47 language code used in "Auto" mode when the text cannot be confidently matched to a known language profile.
aiQueue.allowedStateTypes: Comma-separated list of allowed Znuny state types (e.g. "open" or "open,pending reminder") the AI may set a ticket to via update_ticket_fields — enforced for both the state name and the numeric state id. Empty = default "open" only (reopening allowed, never closing); an explicit empty list "[]" disables state changes entirely.
aiQueue.piiNer: Detects person names in text via spaCy NER (German + English) and masks them as [NAME_n] before content reaches the LLM provider — in addition to customer name and senders. Only active when PII masking is on. False positives are harmless (just more masking).
aiQueue.summaryDetail: Standard: compact paragraphs, 1–2 sentences per attached document. Detailed: more thorough paragraphs and 3–5 sentences per document — useful for queues with many PDF attachments.

common.validId: Valid/invalid is a soft-delete flag: marking an entry invalid hides it from pickers for new records without deleting existing references (e.g. tickets, assignments). There is no hard delete.

groups.name: Groups gate queue access: every queue belongs to exactly one group, and agents, roles, or customer users get read ("ro") or full ("rw") access to it purely through group membership — never directly on the queue.

roles.name: Roles bundle group permissions that are then assigned to many agents at once — editing the role immediately affects every agent who holds it. Roles are optional: groups can also be assigned to agents directly, and both paths are additive (a user gets the union of both).

customerUsers.customerId: This customer user's home company. Access to additional companies is granted separately via "Customer Users ↔ Companies" and does not change this home assignment.
customerUsers.email: Used for notifications (e.g. ticket updates). Independent of the login name — the email address does not need to match the login.

customerCompanies.customerId: The company's unique key, referenced by tickets and customer users. Set on creation — avoid changing it afterwards, since existing references would otherwise no longer resolve.

authConfig.enforceAll: Forces two-factor authentication (TOTP) for every agent account, regardless of the group selection and the per-agent checkboxes below.
authConfig.enforceGroups: Additionally forces 2FA for every agent who is a member of any group checked here — even when "Enforce for all" is off.
authConfig.sso: Allows this agent to authenticate via the external SSO/identity-provider path. Does not configure the SSO provider itself — only whether this agent may use it.
authConfig.enforce: Forces 2FA for this individual agent account, independent of the global settings above.
authConfig.reset: Irreversibly deletes both the agent's TOTP enrollment and all registered passkeys. The agent must set up 2FA from scratch afterwards — there is no soft intermediate state.

queues.group: Determines who can see and work this queue — access is controlled entirely through the group, never granted per queue.
queues.systemAddress: The From address used for outgoing mail from this queue; inbound mail addressed to it is automatically filed into this queue.
queues.followUp: What happens when a customer replies to an already-closed ticket in this queue: depending on the option, the ticket reopens, the reply is rejected, or a brand-new ticket is created instead.
queues.followUpLock: Automatically unlocks a locked ticket back to its owner as soon as a customer follow-up arrives.
queues.calendarName: References a business-hours calendar (holidays, opening times) used when computing escalations. Empty = the default around-the-clock calendar.
queues.defaultSignKey: Name of a PGP/S-MIME key used to automatically sign outgoing mail from this queue. Empty = no automatic signing.

states.typeId: The type — not the name — drives behavior: "new"/"open"/"pending" types count as open tickets, while "merged" hides the ticket from normal search (the record itself is kept). Multiple state names can share the same type.

priorities.name: There is no separate sort-order column: list ordering follows the numeric ID (lower ID = lower priority), not the name.

dynamicFields.name: Internal technical field name (used in placeholders and the API). Cannot be changed after creation.
dynamicFields.fieldType: Determines the input control (text, dropdown, date, …) and cannot be changed after creation, since existing stored values would otherwise lose their meaning.
dynamicFields.objectType: Determines where the field attaches: "Ticket" lives on the whole case, "Article" attaches to a single message within the ticket.
dynamicFields.config: Field-type-specific options — e.g. selectable values for dropdown/multiselect, row count for text areas, year range for date fields.
dynamicFields.fieldOrder: Controls the display order relative to other fields of the same object type — a lower number appears higher up.

acl.match: Conditions under which this ACL applies (e.g. current queue, state, form field). The change on the right is only applied when every criterion listed here matches.
acl.change: The ACL's effect once its conditions match — typically it restricts or forces the choices available in forms (e.g. only certain follow-up states allowed).

mailOutbound.host: SMTP server hostname for outgoing mail. Must be reachable from this server (DNS + firewall) on the port below.
mailOutbound.port: TCP port of the SMTP server — commonly 25 or 587 for STARTTLS, 465 for implicit TLS (SSL).
mailOutbound.security: Transport encryption negotiated with the SMTP server: "None" sends in the clear, "STARTTLS" upgrades a plain connection, "SSL" connects TLS-encrypted from the start.
mailOutbound.authType: "None" sends mail without authenticating (anonymous relay) — only works if the server allows it for this host. "Password" uses SMTP-AUTH with the credentials below.
mailOutbound.fromDefault: Default From: address used when a queue or ticket has no more specific sender configured.
mailOutbound.timeoutSeconds: Connection and response timeout in seconds before a send attempt is aborted and logged as failed.

subjectConfig.hook: The tag prefixing the ticket number in the subject (e.g. "Ticket#"). Left empty, Znuny's own configured hook is used.
subjectConfig.divider: Character(s) between the hook and the ticket number inside the brackets, e.g. "Ticket#123" vs "Ticket# 123".
subjectConfig.subjectFormat: Where the [Hook#Number] tag is placed in the subject: "Left" prefixes it, "Right" appends it, "None" omits it (ticket references then rely on Message-ID threading only).

postmasterFilters.match: Header/value pairs an incoming mail must satisfy for this filter to apply. All rows are ANDed; check "negate" to require the header NOT to match instead.
postmasterFilters.set: X-OTRS-* pseudo-headers written onto the ticket once every match rule above is satisfied — e.g. X-OTRS-Queue to route into a specific queue.

autoResponses.typeId: Numeric ID from Znuny's auto_response_type table, not a picker here: typically 1 = auto reply, 2 = auto reject, 3 = auto follow up, 4 = auto reply/new ticket, 5 = auto remove.
autoResponses.systemAddressId: Numeric ID of the system address (From: sender) used to send this auto response — see its ID in Admin → Queues' system-address picker.

apiKeys.name: Free-text label to tell keys apart — a user can hold any number of API keys at once, e.g. one per integration.
apiKeys.expires: "Unlimited" keys never expire on their own and remain valid until revoked by hand — prefer a fixed expiry unless the integration truly needs a permanent key.

daemons.enabled: Whether the service's scheduled run is active. Services marked "always on" have no toggle — they are core to the app and always run.
daemons.schedule: How often the service runs. Daily services always fire at the fixed UTC time shown; interval-based ones can be edited here and reset back to their built-in default.
daemons.takeover: This service replaces the matching Znuny background task. Keep Znuny's own cron/daemon for it disabled — running both at once means the same work happens twice and can race on the same records.

audit.overview: Every LLM chat-completion call (main model, vision pre-pass, provider connection test) is logged here — success or failure, with the exact payload sent after PII masking. Raw payloads and the PII mapping are only visible on the detail view; reveals are themselves logged.
audit.retention: Audit rows older than this many days are deleted automatically once a day. Range: 1–365 days.

ai.promptParts: The effective system prompt is composed from the base prompt plus the active building blocks in the order shown (base first, then top to bottom). Notes are for small clarifications you can add, disable, or remove quickly without touching the main prompt file.
ai.escalationTester: Checks the rules above against a sample tool result without saving anything — the exact same logic the runtime guard uses before autonomous sending. A hit means autonomous sending would be stopped and escalated.

gdpr.mode: "Anonymize" scrubs personal data but keeps the ticket records and their history (referential integrity intact). "Delete" removes the customer record entirely; ticket data is only removed too if the checkbox below is set.
gdpr.filters: Additional selectors narrow the customer set further — every added filter is ANDed with the logins/companies above and with each other.
gdpr.negate: Toggle between "matches" (customers where the pattern matches) and "matches not" (≠, customers where it does NOT match).
gdpr.validId: "Temp" (valid_id 3) is Znuny's third validity state — typically an account pending confirmation (e.g. unconfirmed self-registration), distinct from plain valid/invalid.

bulk.companyHint: Assign a customer company to {{count}} selected users.

notes.queueCustomFields: Configured variables appear as <OTRS_QUEUE_Name>. <TIQORA_QUEUE_Name> is an equivalent alias. Non-standard fields are only substituted when a data source exists.

postmasterFilters.name: Name
postmasterFilters.stopHelp: When set, no further PostMaster filters are evaluated for the message after this filter matches.
postmasterFilters.matchRequired: At least one Match condition is required.
postmasterFilters.hint: Filters are stored in the shared postmaster_filter table (parallel with Znuny) and apply to inbound email.

webhooks.name: Name
webhooks.secretHelp: Leave blank to keep the current secret when editing.
webhooks.eventsHelp: Comma-separated event types (matches tiqora_event_outbox.event_type). Leave empty to subscribe to all events.

apiKeys.name: Name
apiKeys.hint: Plaintext keys are shown only once. Revoke sets valid=false; delete permanently removes the row.
apiKeys.onceWarning: Shown only once — copy and store it securely now.
apiKeys.onceHint: The plaintext key cannot be retrieved again later.

processes.name: Name
processes.readOnlyNote: Process design must be done in Znuny's admin interface; this is a read-only view.

mailOutbound.title: Outbound email (SMTP)
mailOutbound.description: SMTP settings for agent replies. The password is stored encrypted and never returned by the API.

subjectConfig.title: Ticket number in subject
subjectConfig.description: Prefix and position of the ticket number in outgoing email subjects. Clearing a field re-inherits the live Znuny SysConfig value.

daemons.title: Services
daemons.description: Periodic background services run by the Tiqora worker: enable/disable, interval, and live run status.
daemons.takeoverNote: Mutually exclusive with the matching Znuny daemon task — see docs/parallel-operation.md.
daemons.docsHint: For takeover, rollback, and safety-flag details see docs/parallel-operation.md.

poller.name: Poller
poller.description: Tracks Znuny writes and keeps the search index up to date.

outbox.name: Outbox drain
outbox.description: Drains tiqora_event_outbox: search index, webhooks, live updates.

postmaster.name: Postmaster
postmaster.description: Mail fetch and processing (takeover of Znuny's mail daemon).

escalation.name: Escalation
escalation.description: Recomputes the escalation index and fires Start/Stop/NotifyBefore events.

notifications.name: Notifications
notifications.description: Sends event-driven notifications to agents and customers.

gdpr_retention.name: GDPR retention
gdpr_retention.description: Applies configured retention rules once a day.

gdpr_erasure_purge.name: GDPR erasure purge
gdpr_erasure_purge.description: Removes expired erasure backups once a day.

ai_audit_cleanup.name: AI audit cleanup
ai_audit_cleanup.description: Deletes LLM audit log rows past the configured retention once a day.

mailLog.title: Email communication log
mailLog.subtitle: Inbound and outbound email attempts with status and SMTP detail.
mailLog.subject: Subject
mailLog.empty: No log entries match the filters.

attachments.name: Name
attachments.contentHelp: Pick a file — it is stored base64-encoded. Filename and content type are filled in but can be overridden.

templateAttachments.subtitle: Pick a template and choose which standard attachments are linked to it.
templateAttachments.empty: Pick a template above to edit its attachments.

customerUserGroups.title: Customer users↔Groups

customerUserGroups.empty: Pick a customer user above to edit their groups.

queueTemplates.title: Templates↔Queues

queueTemplates.empty: Pick a queue above to edit its templates.

queueAutoResponses.title: Queues↔Auto responses

queueAutoResponses.noAutoResponses: No valid auto responses exist yet.

queueVariables.name: Name
queueVariables.globalHint: Global variables apply to every queue unless the queue defines its own value for the same name.
queueVariables.queueHint: Queue-specific variables override globals with the same name.
queueVariables.physicalHint: Physical columns on this queue (e.g. domain, phonenumber). A configured variable with the same name overrides the physical value.

customerFields.columnNameHelp: Column from customer_user / customer_company (suggestions from the DB).
customerFields.label: Label

authConfig.title: Authentication / 2FA
authConfig.description: Manage Kerberos SSO eligibility and two-factor authentication enforcement per agent.
authConfig.enforceGroups: Require 2FA for members of these groups
authConfig.name: Name
authConfig.resetConfirmBody: This disables 2FA for {{login}}. They will need to enroll again if enforcement still applies.

gdpr.title: Privacy / GDPR
gdpr.subtitle: Resolve customer users via selectors, preview impact, then anonymize or delete with backup.
gdpr.modeAnonymizeHint: PII is replaced deterministically; rows remain and can be rolled back from backup.
gdpr.modeDeleteHint: Customer users and related links are hard-deleted (with backup). Ticket content is anonymized, not deleted.
gdpr.deleteTicketsHint: Also HARD-DELETE this customer's tickets and all linked data (articles, history, attachments, dynamic fields) — with backup + rollback. Retention-relevant tickets are only warned about, not blocked.
gdpr.previewEmpty: No customer users match this selector.

impact.deleteBody: Tables: {{tables}}. Ticket/article content is anonymized, not removed.

gdpr.confirmAnonymizeBody: Anonymize {{count}} customer users? A backup will be created.
gdpr.confirmDeleteBody: WARNING: {{count}} customer users will be permanently deleted (backup until expiry). Type LÖSCHEN to confirm.
gdpr.rollbackBody: Restore backup for job #{{id}}? The current state will be overwritten.
gdpr.purgeBody: Permanently delete backup for job #{{id}}? Rollback will no longer be possible.

pageDescriptions.groups: Manage permission groups for agents and queues.
pageDescriptions.agentGroups: Assign read/write group permissions to agents.
pageDescriptions.customerCompanies: Manage customer companies and their master data.
pageDescriptions.authConfig: Control SSO eligibility and 2FA enforcement per agent.
pageDescriptions.queues: Configure queues, ownership and escalation times.
pageDescriptions.queueTemplates: Assign templates to the queues they're available in.
pageDescriptions.queueAutoResponses: Assign auto responses to the queues that trigger them.
pageDescriptions.states: Manage ticket states and their state logic.
pageDescriptions.dynamicFields: Manage custom ticket and article fields.
pageDescriptions.acl: Maintain access control lists for actions and fields.
pageDescriptions.processes: Manage ticket workflows and process automation.
pageDescriptions.templates: Manage response templates and text snippets.
pageDescriptions.mailOutbound: Configure the outbound SMTP server and sender address.
pageDescriptions.subjectConfig: Configure the subject format and ticket-number hook.
pageDescriptions.postmasterFilters: Maintain rules for processing incoming mail.
pageDescriptions.webhooks: Manage outbound webhooks for ticket events.
pageDescriptions.apiKeys: Manage API keys for external integrations.
pageDescriptions.daemons: Control the status and intervals of background daemons.
pageDescriptions.gdpr: GDPR requests: access, anonymization and deletion with backup.
pageDescriptions.mailLog: Review inbound and outbound email delivery attempts.
pageDescriptions.customerFields: Maintain placeholder variables for customer users.
pageDescriptions.ai: Manage the AI subsystem's operation mode, disclosure and access control.
pageDescriptions.aiProviders: Manage and test LLM providers (OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic).
pageDescriptions.aiMcp: Manage MCP servers as tool sources for the AI and enable tools.
pageDescriptions.aiQueues: Configure AI behavior per queue: autonomy, caps, escalation.
pageDescriptions.aiAudit: Full audit of every LLM request/response, with PII reveal.
pageDescriptions.aiAcl: AI permissions and quotas per group, role, or agent.

kb.title: Knowledge base
kb.validationError: Please fill in a title, slug, category, and content.
kb.submitError: Could not save this article. Please try again.
kb.publishError: Could not publish this article. Please try again.
kb.publishConfirmBody: This makes the current draft the published version. Continue?

help.category: Groups the article in the knowledge-base sidebar and narrows which articles an AI queue policy can pull in via its category-ID filter.
help.language: The article's content language. Agents and the AI knowledge lookup can filter by it, so mixed-language content should be split into separate per-language articles.
help.state: "Draft"/"In review" are only visible in this editor; only "Published" articles show up in customer-facing search and can be used as AI knowledge context. "Archived" hides it from both without deleting it.
help.tags: Comma-separated tags used for browsing and search — also the hook an AI queue policy's "KB tags" setting matches against to decide which articles feed the model.
help.categoryParent: Places the category as a subcategory. The tree is navigation only — permissions are not inherited; every category has its own groups.
help.categorySlug: URL slug of the category. Leave empty to derive it from the name automatically (collisions get a numeric suffix).
help.categorySort: Sort position within its level — smaller numbers come first.
help.categoryCustomerVisible: Published articles in this category appear in the customer portal. Unchecked keeps the category internal only.

attachments.title: Attachments
attachments.uploadFailed: Could not upload this file. Please try again.
attachments.tooLarge: This file is too large (max 25 MB).

category.title: Categories
category.name: Name
category.groupsHelp: Restrict this category to these groups. Leave empty to make it visible to all agents.
category.noGroups: You have no groups you may assign.
category.empty: No categories yet. Create one to get started.
category.saveError: Could not save this category. Please try again.

kb.editorNoCategories: Saving an article requires a category — none exist yet.
